zh.Blisworth, Northamptonshire, England: 英國諾薩普頓史爾布利斯沃斯
zh.lifeSummary: 當Baron John Wake Ist Baron of Liddell於1268年出生在英國諾薩普頓史爾布利斯沃斯時，他的父親Baldwin Le Wak or Wake32歲，母親Hawise Quincy18歲。他和Joan de Fiennes Briennes至少有1個兒子和1個女兒。他去世於1300年4月10日在英國英格蘭波藍德享年32歲。

zh-hans.Blisworth, Northamptonshire, England: 英国諾薩普頓史爾布利斯沃斯
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Baron John Wake Ist Baron of Liddell于1268年出生在英国諾薩普頓史爾布利斯沃斯时，他的父亲Baldwin Le Wak or Wake32岁，母亲Hawise Quincy18岁。他和Joan de Fiennes Briennes至少有1个儿子和1个女儿。他去世于1300年4月10日在英国英格兰波藍德享年32岁。

nameMeanings.name: Wake
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English:

occupational name for ‘(man of) the watch’, from Middle English wak, wake ‘wake, vigil, watch, period of guard duty’ (Old English wacu, Old Norse vaka). It alternates with the synonymous Middle English wache (Old English wacce), hence the variant Watch. The surname was also interchangeable with Waite (the equivalent Old French term) which, like Wake, was sometimes translated as Latin vigil ‘(one who is) watchful, alert’. Compare the synonymous Vail . When the bearers had high social status, the surname probably denoted an officer with responsibility for the security of a castle, town, or royal person. Compare Wakeman .
variant of   Wack  .

variants.name: Vail
meanings: English: occupational name from Old French de la veille ‘of the watch, watchman’, with loss of de; compare Wake . There has been some confusion with the topographic name Vale and the nickname Veal .
Scottish: shortened and altered form of McPhail , Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Phàil ‘son of Paul’.
Irish: variant of   Veale  .

variants.name: Waite
meanings: English (of Norman origin): occupational name for a watchman, either in a town or castle, from Anglo-Norman French waite ‘watchman, member of the watch’ (of ancient Germanic origin; compare Wachter ). In the Middle Ages the town waits typically combined the functions of watchmen and musicians through being trumpeters or pipers. There may also have been some late confusion with White or Wheat .

nameMeanings.name: Ist
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German and Dutch: nickname for someone who was wise and knowledgeable, from Middle High German list, Dutch list ‘wisdom, ingenuity’.
Dutch: habitational name from Ter List, the name being derived from the plant lis ‘iris, flag’.
Americanized form of Hungarian Liszt, a metonymic occupational name for a miller, from liszt ‘flour’.

nameMeanings.name: Baron
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French, English, Dutch, Polish, Czech, Romanian, and Croatian; Breton (also Le Baron): from Old French barun, baron, Middle English barun, baroun, Middle Dutch baroen, Polish, Czech, Romanian, and dialectal Croatian baron ‘baron’ (i.e. a nobleman ranking below an earl and above a knight), a title of nobility which was in England also awarded to certain freemen of the cities of London and York and of the Cinque Ports. As a surname it is unlikely to be a status name denoting a person of rank. Generally, it referred to service in a baronial household or was acquired as a nickname by a peasant who had ideas above his station or applied to a man showing characteristics of a nobleman. See also 3 and 4 below, compare Barron and Lebaron .
French: habitational name from (Le) Baron, the name of several places in various parts of France. Compare   Lebaron  .
German: status name for a freeman or baron, from barūn ‘imperial or church official’, a loanword in Middle High German from Old French (see 1 above).

variants.name: Barron
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Jesus, Manuel, Francisco, Carlos, Miguel, Raul, Pedro, Ruben, Alfredo, Luis.

Scottish: status name from Scots baron, which in the Highlands denoted a small proprietary landholder. In Angus it alluded specifically to a tenant of one of the small baronies attached to the Abbey of Coupar Angus. The surname was taken by Scottish migrants to Ulster. Compare Baron .
Scottish: shortened form of McBarron, from Scottish Gaelic Mac a' Bharain ‘son of the baron’. See Baron and 1 above.

variants.name: Boron
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Krystyna, Edyta, Leszek, Mieczyslaw, Mieczyslawa, Wladyslaw.
Polish (Boroń): from a pet form of the personal names Borzysław or Borzymir, formed with the element bor ‘to fight’ (from Old Slavic boriti se).
French: habitational name from a place so called in Franche-Comté.

variants.name: Baro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Mario, Manuel, Ana, Angel, Carlos, Cesar, Fernando, Jesus, Jose, Orlando, Ramon, Rosalia.  Italian Guido, Angelo, Carmelo, Federico, Lucio.
Catalan (Baró): status name from baró ‘free man, baron’ (see Baron 1), or a habitational name from a place so named.
Hungarian (Báró): status name from báró ‘baron’. In the form Baró, it could be from a pet form of the personal names Barabás (see Barabas ) or Bertalan.

variants.name: Barona
meanings: Spanish: from the feminine form of Barón ‘baron’ (see Baron ), applied as a nickname or as an occupational name for a member of the household of a baroness.
Italian: probably from the feminine form of   Barone  . The name is concentrated in Sondrio province (Lombardy).

variants.name: Barren
meanings: English: probably a variant of   Baron   or   Barran  .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sung, Jung, Yong, Chang, Kwang, Sang, Dong, Hyun, Jae, Jin, Kyung, Jong, Chong, Chung, Byung, Jeong, Pyong, Myong, Hak, Jin Hwan, Sook, Yeong, Choon.
Korean: variant of   O  .
Japanese: variant of Ō (see O ).

variants.name: O
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Bo Young, Chae, Chong Hwan, Dong Kun, Ho, Hwan, Hyun, Jong, Jung, Ki Soon, Kwang.  Korean Min, Chong, Chol, Jung Ho, Myong, Sa, Sung Kyun.

Korean (오; Chinese character: 吳): historically, there were two Chinese characters for the O surname, but by the late Koryŏ period (14th century AD ), one of them had disappeared. Some records indicate that there are as many as 210 different clans for the remaining character, but only sixteen can be positively documented. All sixteen of these clans originate from the same ancestor, O Ch’ŏm (吳 瞻), who migrated to Korea from China during the reign of Shilla's King Chijŭng (500–514 AD ). O is a common surname throughout Korea. Compare Oh .

nameMeanings.name: Liddell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish and northern English: habitational name from any of various places in the Scottish Borders and Cumbria called Liddel, from the Old English river name Hl̄de, meaning ‘loud’, + Old English dæl ‘valley’.

variants.name: Liddle
meanings: Scottish and northern English: variant of   Liddell  .

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Baron John Wake Ist Baron of Liddell was born in 1268, in Blisworth, Northamptonshire, England, his father, Baldwin Le Wak or Wake, was 32 and his mother, Hawise Quincy, was 18. He had at least 1 son and 1 daughter with Joan de Fiennes Briennes. He died on 10 April 1300, in Cumberland, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 32.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Baron John Wake Ist Baron of Liddell est né en 1268, à Blisworth, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, son père, Baldwin Le Wak or Wake, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Hawise Quincy, avait 18 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille avec Joan de Fiennes Briennes. Il est décédé le 10 avril 1300, à Cumberland, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 32 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Baron John Wake Ist Baron of Liddell 1268 in Blisworth, Northamptonshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Baldwin Le Wak or Wake 32 und seine Mutter Hawise Quincy 18. Er hatte mit Joan de Fiennes Briennes mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 10. April 1300 im Alter von 32 Jahren in Cumberland, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Baron John Wake Ist Baron of Liddell è nato il nel 1268 nel Blisworth, Northamptonshire, England, il suo padre, Baldwin Le Wak or Wake, aveva 32 anni e il suo madre, Hawise Quincy, aveva 18 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia con Joan de Fiennes Briennes. Morì il 10 aprile 1300, nel Cumberland, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 32 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Baron John Wake Ist Baron of Liddellが1268年にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアブリスウオルスで生まれた時，父Baldwin Le Wak or Wakeは32歳，母Hawise Quincyは18歳であった。彼はJoan de Fiennes Briennesとの間に少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1300年4月10日にイギリスイギリスクウンバーランドで32歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Baron John Wake Ist Baron of Liddell(이)가 1268년 영국 노르섬프토실르 블리스워스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Baldwin Le Wak or Wake(은)는 32세였고, 그의 어머니 Hawise Quincy(은)는 18세였다. 그는 Joan de Fiennes Briennes(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1300년 4월 10일 영국 잉글랜드 쿠벌랜드에서 32세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Baron John Wake Ist Baron of Liddell nasceu em 1268, em Blisworth, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Baldwin Le Wak or Wake, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Hawise Quincy, tinha 18 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha com Joan de Fiennes Briennes. Ele faleceu em 10 de abril de 1300, em Cumberland, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 32 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Baron John Wake Ist Baron of Liddell родился в 1268 г. в местности блисворт, нортамптоншир, Англия, его отцу по имени Baldwin Le Wak or Wake было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Hawise Quincy, 18. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь от Joan de Fiennes Briennes. Он умер 10 апреля 1300 г. в местности кумбирланд, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 32 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Baron John Wake Ist Baron of Liddell nació en 1268, en Blisworth, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Baldwin Le Wak or Wake, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Hawise Quincy, tenía 18 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija con Joan de Fiennes Briennes. Murió el 10 de abril de 1300, en Cumberland, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 32 años.
